title: Create one-to-one chat website with unique code-based connections

requirements.summary: Create a user registration system that generates a unique numeric code for each user upon signup
acceptanceCriteria: Each user receives a unique numeric code upon registration
Code is displayed to the user after successful registration
Code can be used to identify and connect with the user

file_operations.description: Create registration page that calls the backend registerUser method with a display name and shows the generated unique numeric code to the user after successful registration.
Add route for /register that renders RegisterPage and wire it into the app routing structure.

requirements.summary: Implement a connection system where users can find and connect with other users by entering their unique code number
acceptanceCriteria: Users can enter another user's code to initiate a connection
System validates the entered code exists
Connection is established only between two specific users

file_operations.description: Create connection page with an input field for entering a target user's unique code and a connect button that calls the backend connectUsers method to establish the connection between the current user and the target user.
Add route for /connect that renders ConnectPage and wire it into the app routing structure.

requirements.summary: Build a one-to-one chat interface where connected users can send and receive text messages in real-time
acceptanceCriteria: Users can send text messages to their connected partner
Messages appear in a conversation thread format
Chat history is preserved and retrievable
Only the two connected users can see their conversation

file_operations.description: Create chat page with a message list display area showing conversation history, a text input field for composing messages, and a send button. Implement polling to fetch new messages periodically from the backend and display them in conversation thread format.
Add route for /chat that renders ChatPage and wire it into the app routing structure.
Create a reusable message bubble component that displays individual messages with visual distinction between sent and received messages, including timestamp and sender information.

requirements.summary: Design a modern, user-friendly chat interface with a clean messaging aesthetic using warm, inviting colors like coral and teal, rounded message bubbles, and clear visual hierarchy for sender/receiver messages
acceptanceCriteria: Chat interface uses a cohesive color scheme with warm accent colors
Message bubbles are clearly distinguishable between sender and receiver
Interface is responsive and works on different screen sizes

file_operations.description: Add CSS custom properties for coral and teal accent colors, define global typography styles for legibility, and establish consistent spacing and border-radius values for rounded message bubbles.
Extend Tailwind theme with custom coral and teal color palette, configure responsive breakpoints, and define spacing utilities for the chat interface design system.
Apply warm color scheme with coral for sent messages and teal for received messages, implement rounded bubble styling, and ensure responsive layout with clear visual hierarchy.
Apply cohesive design system with warm colors, ensure responsive layout for different screen sizes, and implement clear visual hierarchy for the chat interface.

requirements.summary: Display the user's own unique code prominently in the interface so they can share it with others
acceptanceCriteria: User's unique code is visible in the main interface
Code can be easily copied for sharing

file_operations.description: Create a component that prominently displays the user's unique numeric code with a clear label and includes a copy-to-clipboard button for easy sharing.
Integrate UserCodeDisplay component into the chat page header or sidebar so the user's code is always visible during chat sessions.
Add UserCodeDisplay component to the main app layout or navigation area to ensure the user's code is accessible from any page in the application.
